text: Suto zastarale metody dnes uz mimo vedsej podpory ale naprogramovat zbran ktora znici vsetko uz nieje problem predstavme opticky riadenu gravitacne vztlakovi pohon zbran gula dysk zaveseny na magonetickom poli kruhovom dodava informacie opticky gula sa posuva tak ze vztlak zdvihne tazisko a to pada vpred otaca gulou doftedy pokial nieje os potrubia vo zvislej polohe a znova vztlak dviha zavazie az hore kedze mame viac potruby vznika nerovnovaha a gula sa toci vedcou rychlostou a kedze mame pohon s nekonecnym opakovanim mame tu aj perpetu mobile aj s velkou silou ucinost dostatocna na vsetko dokaze vilepsit kedze zavazie moze byt magnet moze okolo potrubia navinut cievki a tu sada na viacerich potrubiach virobit viac energie ako minie gula na pohon a tak pridame motor ktory zrychli otacanie a mametu zbran bez potreby zdrojov energie s akim kolvek vikonom podla potreby dokaze prenikat kamkolvek extremne rychlo manevrovat znici lode tanky bunkre miny za pomoci retazi a za pomoci rezonancii mosty domy bunkre.

author: @stephenroot1012
text: I like the Pigeon Pilot Program. It looks like that if anything it looks like it can be put to more use using today's drones. Modify the stylus and touchscreen and you can have the pigeons pilot a drone remotely so that no matter what happens you can almost always reuse the pilots. I could actually see this become a sport where people with loaded drones piloted by pigeons remotely racing to a target, moving or still, in a competition for speed and accuracy. I would be interested to see something like that. That and how to use messenger pigeons to carry USB drives from one computer to another to help bypass the internet and avoid things like the man in the middle attacks.

author: @Inpropaic
text: Great video but you missed one of the greatest attempts at using animals in warfare. Towards the end of the Second World War, Hungary experimented with using mosquitos impregnated with syphilis to infect the enemy, which lead to an outbreak of syphilis in the Hungarian ranks and a swift end to the project. War, what a foolish endeavour.

author: @rogerc7960
text:
Animal senses make good biotech chipsets. 
For example deepsea fish retinas can vision x-ray/microwave/neutrons, eg. satellite imagery.
Smell biochips can spectrograph identify explosives/hazmat.
Balance biochips are used in quadcopters.
Elephant feet are better at vibration sensing through the ground.
